## Recommended next task

(Extracted from the report's Phase 11 recommendation)

Add `?bpCanvasLegacy=1` as the inverse kill-switch and make the proven stack
default-on.

1. In `barPatternDebug.js`, change `bpPrimitiveHybridEnabled()` to return `true`
   by default, unless `?bpCanvasLegacy=1` is present in the URL.
   Keep all granular flags and `?bpPrimitive=1` as overrides.
   Keep `?bpHybrid=1` unchanged (bodies/wicks only).

2. Pre-flip gate (human, ~2 min): open the app with NO flags. Verify:
   - Primitive renders bodies/wicks automatically.
   - P1/P2 handles appear.
   - Drag/resize/lock/context-menu all work.
   - Console is clean.
   Then open `?bpCanvasLegacy=1` and confirm the old canvas path is restored.

3. Run `npm run build`. Fix any errors. Verify 0 errors.

4. Test Transform 2x2 with no flags (default-on). Confirm all 4 panes work,
   no leaks, no "Object is disposed" errors.

Important: Do NOT retire any flags yet. Do NOT change BarPatternCanvas.jsx,
barPatternPrimitive.js, useBarPatternPrimitive.js, or any reducer/storage code.
Work only in `src/drawings/bar-pattern/barPatternDebug.js`.

## Verification checklist

After Claude finishes, verify:

1. App with no flags: primitive renders bar pattern bodies (no canvas bodies).
2. App with `?bpCanvasLegacy=1`: canvas renders everything (legacy path restored).
3. All interaction still works with no flags: drag, resize, lock, context menu.
4. Transform 2x2 with no flags: all 4 panes, 0 leaks, clean console.
5. `npm run build` passes with 0 errors.
6. Old granular flags (`?bpHybrid=1`, `?bpPrimitiveHandles=1`, etc.) still work standalone.
